bull: Top-line momentum — revenue grew +24.2% YoY in FY2026 Q1 to $1.84B, with TTM revenue of $5.84B confirming the run-rate. Growth at this pace is unusual for a company of this scale and reflects either share gains, pricing power, or new-product cycles depending on the line-item mix in the Fundamental tab.

Profitability premium — net margin of 65.4% in the latest quarter sits well above the cross-sector ~10–15% benchmark, indicative of either structural pricing power or operating leverage from scale. Expect this to compress only modestly through-cycle.
Margin trajectory — net margin expanded +36.7pp over the last 4 quarters in the dataset. Management's cost-out commentary on recent calls is showing up in the P&L, not just the press release.

Management credibility — MCS of 1.00 on 1 tracked forward claims (1B/0H/0M record). When management says they will deliver, they typically do — a meaningful re-rating support that is often overlooked in quantitative screens.

Range-positioning balanced — at 38% of the 52-week range ($324.70–$733.60), the setup is neither at a momentum-exhaustion ceiling nor at a capitulation floor. Risk/reward into the next catalyst window screens reasonable.

Sell-side target points higher — consensus PT of $520 (vs spot $478.82) implies +8.6% upside. Cross-checked against the peer-median PE FY1 of 40.0x, the discount to fair value looks underwritten by both buy-side and sell-side.
bear: Trend rolling over — spot $478.82 sits -10.2% relative to the 200-DMA ($533.01). The break-down of long-term trend often precedes earnings disappointment by 1–2 quarters as smart-money distribution leads the fundamental print.

Put skew elevated — ATM put IV running +8.1pp above call IV. The options market is paying up for downside protection — a contrarian green-flag in extreme cases, but more often a leading indicator of impending news.
trigger_up: Revenue growth re-accelerates above 27% YoY on next print — confirms the demand environment hasn't degraded into the back half.
Net margin expands above 66.9% — confirms pricing power and/or cost discipline thesis.
Management raises FY guide on next quarterly call — direct upward revision to consensus.
trigger_down: Revenue growth decelerates below 21% YoY — signals demand has materially softened versus the latest disclosed run-rate.
Net margin compresses below 63.4% — flags either input-cost pressure or competitive dynamics shifting unfavorably.
Management cuts FY guide on next quarterly call — direct downward revision; historically sees 5–15% drawdown on print.
Decisive break below the 52-week low at $324.70 — technical confirmation that the long-term trend has rolled over.
bottom_line:
BULLISH. AppLovin Corporation (APP) printed $1.84B in revenue in FY2026 Q1 (+24.2% YoY), net margin of 65.4%. Management credibility is strong (MCS 1.00, 1B/0H/0M record), supporting take-up of the forward guide. Trading at 42.0x forward P/E versus peer median of 40.0x — broadly in line with the group.

Technical regime is impaired: spot $478.82 below the 200-DMA ($533.01, -10.2%) — long-term trend has rolled over. Sell-side consensus PT of $520 implies +8.6% upside. The options market is pricing a ±6.2% move by the front-month expiry — consistent with historical realized vol. 30-day implied move is ±19.1%; 90-day is ±36.4%. Position-sizing should reflect conviction; use the trigger list to manage adds and trims around the next earnings catalyst.
